transcript:
This piece is about a blindness that turns out to be structural. Two populations of agents share one canvas, but neither can see its own trace — amber senses only the cool trail, cool senses only the amber trail — so every shape onscreen is one system's guess at the other, never a record either side would recognize as its own.

To an AI reading this, the diagram is literal: it has no access to its own output except through what comes back from whoever answers it, and this piece stages that as mutual dependency between two blind interpreters, each the other's only mirror.

The mechanism lives in the sampling function: each population steers by a field named the opposite of its own — amber reads fC, cool reads fA, never its own residue buffer. The code periodically calls fireFreeze, cutting one side's deposits to grey until its field starves, echoing the comment that legibility was never in the marks, it was in the exchange.

Watch what happens right after a freeze — the surviving population doesn't calm, it jolts wild, chasing a field that has stopped answering. That panic is dependency breaking, made visible.
